touches.ai_summary: Inbound request received from Savannah Park (Head of Sales) at OrbitalBridge. Expressed interest in automation tools to improve rep productivity. Scheduled qualification call.
Brief qualification call with Savannah. Confirmed 5-person sales team struggling with manual data entry and follow-up tasks. Budget discussion deferred to next call.

questions_asked.question: What's the biggest productivity challenge your team faces right now?
questions_asked.answer: Manual CRM updates and follow-up reminders are eating up 8-10 hours per week per rep

next_steps: Deep dive call to explore specific workflows

touches.ai_summary: Full qualification call. Savannah detailed current workflow gaps and timeline pressure to get solution in place before Q3. Discussed unlimited automation tier. No other decision-makers mentioned yet.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this solution?
questions_asked.answer: I'll handle the evaluation and bring in our CFO closer to contract time if needed

questions_asked.question: Does the unlimited automation tier include API access for custom integrations?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, full API access is included

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to address Savannah's questions about integrations with their existing PropTech stack. Discussed ROI calculations. Savannah mentioned tight budget constraints but wants to move forward.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down the pricing by seat vs automation features?
questions_asked.answer: Base is $430/seat annually, unlimited automation adds $1,950 to total package

questions_asked.question: What's your budget range for this initiative?
questions_asked.answer: We're looking at around $2,000-$2,500 for the team this year

objections: Initial pricing is above our allocated budget

touches.ai_summary: Demo follow-up email. Savannah confirmed the team loved the demo and wants to proceed. Requested formal proposal with adjusted pricing to fit budget constraints.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on pricing given we're a smaller team?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss discount options with my manager and get back to you

next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing options

touches.ai_summary: Sent proposal with adjusted pricing at $3,100 ACV (35.9% discount) to meet budget. Savannah reviewing internally and will respond by end of week.
next_steps: Follow up Friday if no response

touches.ai_summary: Check-in email on proposal status. Savannah responded that pricing works but needs a few more days to finalize internally. Still no mention of executive involvement.

questions_asked.question: Is there anything blocking moving forward on your end?
questions_asked.answer: Just need to coordinate with finance on the payment schedule

next_steps: Schedule call to discuss contract terms

touches.ai_summary: Pre-negotiation email confirming call scheduled to finalize terms. Savannah mentioned wanting to discuss payment terms and implementation support.

Negotiation call focused on payment terms and implementation timeline. Savannah requested quarterly payment option and dedicated onboarding support. Agreed to annual payment with extended onboarding. Contract to be sent this week.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Annual payment is required for this discount level, but we can offer extended payment terms

questions_asked.question: What level of implementation support is included?
questions_asked.answer: Standard tier includes email support; we can add dedicated onboarding sessions

questions_asked.question: When do you need this implemented by?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally before end of Q3, so we have some flexibility

objections: Prefer quarterly payment structure for cash flow management
next_steps: Send final contract with extended payment terms
